observations.recommendation:
1.9 We recommended and Management agreed to direct:
a. The Inventory Committee:
i. To conduct a complete physical count annually of all property, plant
and equipment of the municipality and submit the corresponding
reports;
ii. To reconcile the results of the physical inventory with the accounting
and property records; and
iii. In coordination with the Municipal Treasurer and Property Officer, to
ensure that all properties are properly tagged with property stickers
on the prominent but secure portion of the property for easy and
immediate identification.
b. The Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, and the Inventory
Committee to exert utmost effort to determine the specific asset
composition of the properties with “unreconciled” account names.

1.10 We also recommended and Management agreed that, in compliance with the
provisions of COA Circular No. 2015-008 on the recognition of the Local Road
Network in the books of accounts, require:
a. The Inventory Committee to expedite the conduct of the physical inventory
of road networks acquired in 2016 and prior years under the Municipality’s
jurisdiction; and
b. The Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Engineering
Office regarding the description and cost segregation of road components
for the unrecognized local road networks, and to prepare the appropriate
journal entries to recognize the local road network components in the
books.

2.7 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
a. Prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to reclassify the
disbursement amounting to ₱998,578.46 from Repairs and Maintenance –
Buildings and Other Structures to the Buildings account, and adjust the
related PPE and equity accounts accordingly; and
b. Determine the appropriate useful life of the building and compute the
corresponding depreciation beginning the time the building became
available for use.

3.6 We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Direct the Inventory Committee and the Municipal Accounting Office to
reconcile their respective Report on the Physical Count of Local Road
Network and the Local Road Network Ledger Card;
b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant:
i. To ensure that depreciation is not applied to road lot component of the
road network system, pursuant to Item IV (4) of COA Circular 2015-008;
and
ii. Provide depreciation for all depreciable components, as required by Item
IV (13) of COA Circular 2015-008 and ensure consistent application of
depreciation policy.
iii. Prepare the proposed adjusting entry:
Account Debit Credit
Accumulated Depreciation – Road Networks ₱317,052.46
Prior Period Adjustment ₱317,052.46

4.7 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the
necessary adjusting journal entries to reclassify from Leased Assets – Land
to Prepaid Rent the lease payment amounting to the ₱100,000.00, and to
correct other accounts affected, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-
009 and Section 56 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs.

5.7 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to locate the unaccounted inventory items worth
₱309,100.00 and ensure that the stock cards are updated accordingly; and
b. Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Accountant, and Inventory Committee to
ensure reconciliation of their inventory records after every physical count,
and immediately resolve any discrepancies.

6.20 We recommended and Management agreed to refrain from entering into land
acquisition transactions at prices significantly higher than market valuations
without adequate justification and supporting documentation, to ensure
prudent use of public funds and compliance with rules against excessive,
extravagant, or unnecessary expenditures under COA Circular No. 2012-003.

6.21 We also recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Provide valid justification and/or submit legal basis supporting the
acquisition of the land at the purchase price of ₱14,500,000.00, which is
significantly higher than the appraised market values; and
b. Submit feasibility study for the land acquisition and the proposed
development of a public market and multi-purpose gymnasium, or any
related projects under the Municipality’s development plan, as prescribed
under Section 1.E, Chapter VIII of the Manual on Real Property Appraisal
and Assessment Operations.

7.7 We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Direct the Municipal Engineering Office (MEO) to ensure the timely
preparation of the Program of Works, taking into account procurement
schedules and implementation timelines;
b. Instruct the Municipal Engineering Office to closely monitor and expedite
the implementation of the uncompleted PPAs to reduce disaster risks,
mitigate socioeconomic and environmental impacts, and enhance
community disaster preparedness and response; and
c. Require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council, in
the formulation of the LDRRMF Investment Plan, to determine and decide
on the appropriate use of the unexpended balance from previous years’
Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund, while ensuring
compliance with the five-year programming requirement for Special Trust
Funds.

8.8 We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Henceforth, strictly comply with Section 294 of the Local Government Code
of 1991 by ensuring that 80 percent of the share from national wealth is used
exclusively to reduce electricity costs, while the remaining portion is
allocated for development and livelihood projects; and
b. Ensure strict adherence to COA Circular No. 92-382, particularly in
determining whether funds qualify as idle before placing them in time
deposit accounts.

9.9 We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Direct the Municipal Engineering Office (MEO) and the Municipal
Development Council (MDC) to address the issues for the non-
implementation of the projects and develop effective solutions to ensure
the timely implementation of infrastructure projects;
b. Ensure the timely identification of priority projects to be funded by the
MDF, allowing sufficient time for the procurement process and project
implementation.

9.10 We further reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation and management
agreed that the MDC ensure that programs, projects and activities included in
the Annual Investment Plan are well-planned, procurement and
implementation-ready, and sufficiently funded to enable the management to
procure the required infrastructure projects promptly within a reasonable
time.

10.7 We recommended and Management agreed to immediately return the total
amount of ₱2,244,369.22 to the National Wealth Share Current Account to
rectify the improper fund transfers; and

10.8 We also recommended and Management agreed to establish and implement
adequate internal control measures to prevent similar errors, such as
maintaining a fund tracking register or verification procedures for inter-fund
transfers.

11.8 We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Ensure that legal rights over land are properly secured prior to the
construction of infrastructure projects, to safeguard government assets
against loss or wastage and to avoid legal issues that may arose; and
b. Strictly adhere to the provisions of Annex “H” of the Revised IRR of R.A.
No. 9184 in future lease agreements involving privately-owned real
property.

16.7 We recommended that Management require the officials concerned to comply
with laws, rules and regulations to avoid audit suspensions, disallowances,
and charges and settle the same within the prescribed period to prevent their
accumulation.

and reporting guidelines on the Local Road Asset Management System
prescribed by COA Circular No. 2015-008 in order to come up with a

Accountant to:

ensure that no depreciation shall be provided for the lot component of

consideration the following factors; a) design; b) users and c) volume
of users; and

prescribe range of useful life and to prepare the necessary adjusting
entries in order to present fairly the financial statements.

adjusting entries to correct the balance of the Road Network account and
Accumulated Depreciation – Road Network account, specifically for the
road opening.

Account Debit Credit
Accumulated Depreciation – Road Network 1,942,026.14
Government Equity 1,942,026.14

adjusting entry to correct the depreciation provided for road opening was
prepared and submitted to the Audit Team on May 20, 2022.

a) Impose liquidated damages upon contractors who incurred

We recommended that Management require the MSB to provide the list of intended beneficiaries in the project proposal and to submit the duly signed list of recipients of USB speaker, flash drive and LED.
We further recommended that the BAC provide an explanation pertaining to the overpriced items and to ensure that the most advantageous price is availed of.
The BAC justified that the price was reasonable since the local supplier took into consideration the shipping fees, transportation expense and mark-up. That the price in the box was not the actual buying price but higher due to high demand of wifi and low supply. The BAC alleged time is of the essence, hence it negotiated the procurement to a local supplier who was willing to provide the needed quantity as soon as possible inspite of the scarcity of supply of wifi.
The Audit Team could not consider the explanation given, thus, we stand with our recommendation. The price offer of ₱1,680.00 per unit taking into consideration shipping fees, transportation expense and mark-up of 69 percent when compared to the selling price of ₱999.00 per unit or ₱681.00 difference was considered unreasonable.
The BAC did not exert its effort to get quotation from other stores in Baguio City or from direct distributors of prepaid WIFI like Globe or Smart. In addition, BAC could have asked for request for quotation to suppliers electronically as a remedy for restriction brought about by COVID 19. Moreover, the reason that time is of the essence is untenable as the local supplier was given 20 days from receipt of the Notice to Proceed to deliver the items.  Further, the items were delivered on September 24, 2020 which is more than one month from the date of the Purchase Request and BAC Resolution adopting the CY 2020 Supplemental Annual Procurement Plan including the method of procurement which is August 11, 2020.

observations.recommendation:
1.9 We recommended and Management agreed to the following actions:
a. Exert all efforts to collect the receivables account to the concerned
debtors/persons liable by sending them Demand Letters or follow-
up letters;
b. File the necessary request for the write-off of dormant receivables
that are deemed uncollectible complete with supporting documents
evidencing that efforts to collect the same were made;
c. Diligently make efforts to locate the necessary supporting
documents establish the existence and validity for claiming the
receivables and to cause their collection; and
d. Advise the Municipal Accountant to provide Allowance for
Impairment for the receivables account so as to present fairly the
proper valuation of their net realizable value, and, adjust its effect
to the equity account.

2.7 We recommended and Management agreed to require the General
Services Office the following:
a. Conduct physical count of Inventory items semestrally and prepare a
RPCI and submit to the Auditor not later than July 31 and January 31
of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively;
b. Perform semestral reconciliation of the RCPI with the balance of the
Inventory account as recorded by the Municipal Accounting Office;
and
c. Submit SSMI to support the distribution or issuances of inventories.

3.5 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record all regular
purchases of supplies under the proper inventory accounts following the
perpetual inventory method. Issuances shall be taken up as expense based
on the SSMI prepared by the Municipal Treasurer.

4.6 We recommended that Management:
 Direct the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to immediately prepare and
provide the Disposal Committee a copy of the duly accomplished and
signed IIRUP for the unserviceable properties;
 Require the Disposal Committee to promptly evaluate the
unserviceable properties and recommend to the Local Chief Executive
the appropriate mode of disposal; and
 Ensure that disposal of unserviceable properties conform to the
guidelines on disposal.

5.7 We recommended and Management agreed to ensure that local budgets
shall operationalize approved local development plans pursuant to Section
305(i) of the Local Government Code of 1991 by maximizing the use of
available funds for optimum results in the achievement of the
municipality’s vision towards the upliftment of the quality of life of the
people.

6.9 We recommended and the Municipal Engineer agreed to: a) submit the
bidding documents for the Construction of H.E. Building; b) ensure the
timely completion of project in accordance with the agreed target time of
completion; and c) strictly monitor and supervise the accomplishment and
timely implementation of all infrastructure project

7.13 We recommended that Management:
a. Submit to the Audit Team the written notice or request for extension of
contract time and all other supporting documents as prescribed in the
Revised IRR of R.A. 9184, for evaluation; and
b. Ensure that, prior to granting suspension of work and extension of
contract time: (1) the grounds cited by the Contractor are among those
enumerated in the Revised IRR of R.A. 9184, and (2) requests are
justified by the circumstances via an investigation/determination of its
applicability.

8.4 We recommended and Management agreed to provide, in every contract, a
provision on contract time and liquidated damages which shall be payable
by the contractor in case of breach thereof; and observe strictly the
guidelines in the implementation of infrastructure contracts as stated in the
Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.

9.9 We recommended and Management agreed to require the GAD Focal
persons to a) provide the breakdown or details of projects and activities
and their corresponding budget under the different attributed programs;
and b) submit the result of the HGDG assessment together with the
supporting documents as required in the PCW, DBM, DILG and NEDA
JMC No. 2016-01.

10.8 We commended the Municipal Treasurer’s Office in surpassing the annual
collections in revenues over last year’s figures. Nevertheless, for Business
Tax and Tax on Sand, Gravel and Other Quarry Products that reported a
significant decreased in collection, we suggest Management to conduct
review of its collections efficiency to ensure improved performance and
higher revenue generations. Also, Management should consider systematic
and realistic forecasting taking into account previous years’ experience and
statistics as aid in setting targets.
